responses.content: Did you want to know some cool facts about how 2 people changed the entire world just by standing for theirselves and others? Well here’s some so it will be a cool story.Claudette colvin’s actions were her fighting for her rights. And Barbara’s actions were striking for the rights of students education. In the story of Freedom walkers also works with many people. While in Claudettes situation only she stood up for other black riders and herself. The author develops the idea that Claudette actions were standing up for other black riders because she had the right to sit in her seat because she has been learning about the U.S Constitution. “ The white boy told Marshall to get up from the seat beside him. Marshall refused. Then the bus driver ordered the black teenagers to move, but they continued to sit where they were. Up North, they were accustomed to riding integrated buses and trains. They didn’t see now why they should give up their seats”. “ Claudette didn’t move. She knew she wasn’t sitting in the restricted white section. She felt that she was far enough back to be entitled to her seat. A pregnant black woman was sitting next to her. When the driver insisted that the woman get up and stand in the aisle, a black man in the rear offered her his seat, then quickly left the bus to avoid trouble.” The main idea in freedom walkers is people standing up for theirselves. In freedom Because Claudette refused to get out of her seat because she thought that they cant force her to get out of her seat. And in freedom daughters she worked with many people to protest because they wanted better education. This teaches us that it is not impossible to gain equality. This story shows that people can stand up for other people if they dont like what they want because either their education is poor or something else that is poor that kids need.
